title: Joint pain, rash and fever in a 24-year-old female
presentation: A 24-year-old female presents with 2 weeks of symmetric joint pain, a facial rash that worsens in sunlight, fatigue, and mouth sores. She now has fever and shortness of breath. Vitals: BP 142/90, HR 104, RR 22, Temp 38.6C, SpO2 95%. Exam: butterfly malar rash sparing nasolabial folds, swollen wrists and MCP joints, oral ulcers, decreased breath sounds at right base, mild pericardial rub.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Malar rash + serositis + arthritis + renal involvement = SLE. Check urinalysis for casts — lupus nephritis changes management. ANA + anti-dsDNA + low complement = active disease. Hydroxychloroquine + steroids for flare.
